questions.q: Who is eligible to vote in U.S. federal elections?
options: U.S. citizens who meet age and residency rules
questions.explanation: Voting in federal elections is reserved for citizens, generally 18 and older.

questions.q: What must most people do before they can vote?
questions.explanation: Most states require voter registration; start at vote.gov.

questions.q: What is the difference between a primary and a general election?
options: A primary chooses a party's nominee; the general election chooses the officeholder
A primary is only for president
A general election picks party leaders
questions.explanation: Primaries narrow the field; the general election fills the office.

options: A federal election held between presidential elections
questions.explanation: Midterms decide House seats, a third of the Senate, and many state/local offices.

questions.q: Besides the President, who else do voters elect?
options: Senators, representatives, governors, and many state and local officials
questions.explanation: Ballots include many offices at every level.
